READ-ONLY investigation. cd /home/polaris/wt/flywheel. Do NOT edit any file.
CONTEXT: We will RELAX the synthesis classifier to admit more cross-source verdicts, but we MUST NOT weaken attribution/anti-fabrication faithfulness. Your job is to draw the exact safety boundary.
HOW: Read scripts/synthesis_contract.py, scripts/report_ast.py, scripts/argument_planner.py, scripts/publisher.py. Enumerate EVERY guard/check that enforces ATTRIBUTION or ANTI-FABRICATION faithfulness (correct source per claim; no number/unit that isn't in the source span; no smuggled new finding; premises must be already-admitted cards). For each: file:line, what it rejects, and WHY it is load-bearing.
Specifically classify these drop reasons as SACRED (keep) vs GHOST (relaxable): JUDGE_NOT_ENTAILED, NUMBER_OR_UNIT_NOT_IN_SPAN, SOURCE_NAMED_IN_CLAUSE_TEXT, UNPROVED_RIDER_CLAUSE, OWNED_VERDICT_UNCLASSIFIABLE, OWNED_NAMES_A_SOURCE, OWNED_ATTRIBUTES_A_FINDING, SYNTHESIS_REFUSED:premises_share_a_single_source, SYNTHESIS_SMUGGLES_A_FINDING, OWNED_ASSERTS_UNLICENSED_FINDING, SYNTHESIS_REFUSED:new_entity.
Also: find scripts/synthesis_contract.py's --self-test (it must stay GREEN: 9 invalid/0 false admissions, 5 valid/0 false rejections) and describe what the 9 invalid cases are — these define what a safe relaxation must STILL reject.
DELIVER: the SACRED list (must-not-touch, with file:line), the GHOST list (safe-to-relax), and the exact invariant a new 'fallback verdict type' must preserve so the 9 invalid self-test cases stay rejected.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""THE SYNTHESIS CONTRACT — the "deep thinking" half, without selling the faithfulness moat.
3	
4	THE PROBLEM, PRECISELY
5	----------------------
6	POLARIS's entailment gate (entailment_judge.py:588) kills any sentence that introduces
7	"a fact, entity, MECHANISM ... NOT present in the SPAN". A cross-source inference IS, by definition,
8	a mechanism present in neither span. So our own gate deletes exactly the sentence type that earns
9	INSIGHT — 0.32 weight, the heaviest dimension, and our worst score (0.4238 vs bodhi's 0.5457).
10	
11	We are not bad at analysis. We built a machine that cannot do it.
12	
13	THE INSIGHT THAT MAKES THIS SOLVABLE
14	------------------------------------
15	Look at what the winning systems' synthesis prose actually asserts. cellcog, verbatim:
16	
17	    "The three frameworks are complementary rather than competitive. SBTC remains the clearest model
18	     for relative skill demand. The task-based framework subsumes the phenomena both describe within
19	     a more general model of endogenous task allocation."
20	
21	NO new fact. NO number. NO new entity. It RANKS and RELATES claims already on the page.
22	That is ADJUDICATION, and it is fully compatible with a no-fabrication guarantee.
23	
24	So we do not relax the gate. We add a SECOND lane with a DIFFERENT PROOF OBLIGATION:
25	
26	    EVIDENCE sentence  -> must be span-grounded in ONE source (the existing gate, UNCHANGED)
27	    SYNTHESIS sentence -> must be a TYPED RELATION over premises that are THEMSELVES already admitted
28	
29	A synthesis sentence may not introduce a fact; it may only assert a relationship between facts that
30	are already proven. Its proof obligation is structural, not evidential.
31	
32	THE HARD RULE THAT KEEPS US HONEST
33	----------------------------------
34	    An explanatory MECHANISM may appear ONLY if a premise span states that mechanism.
35	    Otherwise POLARIS may identify a contrast but MAY NOT explain its cause.
36	
37	So this is legal:
38	    "Firm-level expansion and worker-level displacement are not contradictory: the two estimates
39	     observe different units of analysis."          [CONTRAST_LEVEL over two admitted premises]
40	And this is NOT, unless 'adoption lag' is stated in a premise:
41	    "The difference probably reflects slower regional adoption."   [imports an unproven mechanism]
42	
43	Run the adversarial suite:  python scripts/synthesis_contract.py --self-test
44	"""
45	from __future__ import annotations
46	
47	import argparse
48	import json
49	import re
50	import sys
51	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
52	from pathlib import Path
53	
54	# ---------------------------------------------------------------- the typed operations
55	
56	OPERATIONS = {
57	    'CONVERGES':            'two or more premises agree on direction/finding',
58	    'CONTRASTS_DIRECTION':  'premises disagree on the direction of an effect',
59	    'CONTRASTS_LEVEL':      'premises differ because they observe different units (task/worker/firm/region/economy)',
60	    'CONTRASTS_HORIZON':    'premises differ because they observe different time horizons',
61	    'CONTRASTS_METHOD':     'premises differ because they use different identification strategies',
62	    'BOUNDARY_CONDITION':   'a premise holds only within a stated scope',
63	    'RANK_EVIDENCE':        'one premise rests on a stronger design than another (declared fields only)',
64	    'ESTABLISHES':          'the admitted evidence supports a claim AT A STATED LEVEL',
65	    'DOES_NOT_ESTABLISH':   'the admitted evidence does NOT support a claim at a stated level',
66	    'REMAINS_UNRESOLVED':   'the admitted evidence cannot distinguish between the competing accounts',
67	    'COVERAGE_GAP':         'a cell of the evidence matrix is empty — a real, derivable research gap',
68	    # ---- RUNG 4 verdict operations, each with a proof rule in prove():
69	    'RECONCILES':           'opposed results are simultaneously true because their span-bound scopes differ',
70	    'BOUNDARY':             'a pure limitation of the evidence — asserts no relation between premises',
71	}
72	
73	# The rubric rewards ADJUDICATION, not hedging. This is the verdict vocabulary.
74	VERDICT_VOCAB = (
75	    'establishes', 'does not establish', 'supports', 'is limited to', 'cannot distinguish',
76	    'remains unresolved', 'are not contradictory', 'is consistent with', 'rests on',
77	    'observe different', 'holds only', 'no evidence', 'the evidence cannot', 'differ because',
78	    'complementary rather than', 'subsumes', 'rather than',
79	)
80	
81	# A synthesis sentence may NEVER assert a causal mechanism of its own.
82	CAUSAL_IMPORT = re.compile(
83	    r'\b(because of|due to|caused by|driven by|reflects|reflecting|owing to|as a result of|'
84	    r'attributable to|explains why|the reason is|stems from|arises from|leads to|results in)\b', re.I)
85	
86	# Forecasts / predictions are always fabrication in a literature review.
87	FORECAST = re.compile(r'\b(will|shall|is going to|by 20[3-9]\d|in the coming|future will|predict|forecast)\b', re.I)
88	
89	# Universal quantifiers = overclaim.
90	UNIVERSAL = re.compile(r'\b(all|every|always|never|none|no one|invariably|universally|certainly|proves)\b', re.I)
91	
92	NUMERIC = re.compile(r'\d')
93	SPELLED_QTY = re.compile(
94	    r'\b(doubl|tripl|quadrupl|halv|tenfold|twofold|threefold|a third|a quarter|a half|two[- ]thirds|'
95	    r'percent|percentage|majority|most of|vast majority)\w*\b', re.I)
96	CAP_TOKEN = re.compile(r"\b([A-Z][A-Za-z&.\-']{1,})\b")
97	
98	# ── SOL P1 — THE SAFE_CAPS AUTHORITY IS DELETED FROM PRODUCTION VALIDATION ─────────────────────────
99	# A capitalised token used to clear the "new entity" gate by matching a HAND-CURATED set (SAFE_CAPS).
100	# V11 already stripped the task-72 entities from it; P1 finishes the job: there is NO subject allow-list.
101	# A capitalised name is permitted ONLY when it is (a) present in a premise, (b) present in the original
102	# QUESTION / compiled contract, (c) sentence-initial by orthography, or (d) emitted from a TYPED RENDERER
103	# ENUM — an operation name, a verdict marker, a declared facet field, or an epistemic tag. Those four are
104	# structural signals, not a list of fashionable nouns, so the same predicate protects a clinical, a legal
105	# and a CS review with no per-domain edit.
106	#
107	# `RENDERER_VOCAB` is DERIVED (not typed by hand) from the module's own typed enums: the operations, the
108	# verdict vocabulary, the declared `Premise` facet fields, and the epistemic tags the composer renders.
109	# Adding a new operation or facet extends it automatically; adding a world-entity to it is impossible,
110	# because none of those enums names one.
111	_EPISTEMIC_TAGS = ('Established', 'Contested', 'Unresolved', 'Emerging', 'Conceptual', 'Analytical',
112	                   'Hypothesis', 'Pattern', 'Synthesis', 'Proposed', 'Argument')
113	#: pure grammar/discourse: pronouns, determiners, conjunctions, small numbers, connectives. None of these
114	#: is a world-entity, and a fabricated NAME is still caught because it appears in none of the four signals.
115	_DISCOURSE_CAPS = {
116	    'the', 'this', 'these', 'those', 'taken', 'together', 'both', 'neither', 'either', 'while',
117	    'whereas', 'although', 'because', 'when', 'where', 'if', 'in', 'at', 'by', 'for', 'a', 'an',
118	    'it', 'they', 'their', 'its', 'however', 'yet', 'still', 'across', 'within', 'among', 'between',
119	    'one', 'two', 'three', 'four', 'five', 'six', 'seven', 'eight', 'nine', 'ten', 'our', 'we',
120	    'that', 'what', 'nor', 'read', 'seen', 'and', 'but', 'not', 'are', 'than', 'then', 'thus',
121	    'therefore', 'hence', 'also', 'only', 'more', 'most', 'less', 'least', 'former', 'latter',
122	    'whether', 'which', 'does', 'cannot', 'them', 'with', 'without', 'about', 'over', 'under',
123	}
124	
125	
126	#: the DECLARED facet fields (they mirror `Premise`'s dataclass fields — the axes a verdict may turn on),
127	#: plus the evidence-meta nouns the renderers emit. Every one describes the EVIDENCE, not a world-entity.
128	_FACET_WORDS = ('level', 'unit', 'analysis', 'scope', 'design', 'horizon', 'method', 'estimate',
129	                'outcome', 'direction', 'modality', 'comparator', 'population', 'mechanism', 'source',
130	                'premise', 'finding', 'result', 'evidence', 'study', 'studies', 'research',
131	                'literature', 'economy', 'aggregate', 'firm', 'worker', 'task', 'region', 'occupation',
132	                'industry', 'sector')
133	
134	
135	def _renderer_vocab() -> set[str]:
136	    words: set[str] = set()
137	    for phrase in (list(OPERATIONS) + list(VERDICT_VOCAB) + list(_EPISTEMIC_TAGS) + list(_FACET_WORDS)):
138	        for w in re.findall(r'[A-Za-z]+', str(phrase)):
139	            if len(w) >= 2:
140	                words.add(w.lower())
141	    return words
142	
143	
144	def caps_earned(token: str, *, premise_caps: set[str], premise_blob_lower: str,
145	                premise_stems: set[str], context_lower: str = '',
146	                context_caps: frozenset[str] | set[str] = frozenset()) -> bool:
147	    """THE single structural predicate for "is this capitalised token NOT a fabricated world-entity?".
148	
149	    Both `validate()` (the deterministic gate) and `argument_planner.owned_is_safe()` (the owned-voice
150	    gate) call THIS — there is exactly one caps rule, not two allow-lists that can drift apart. True iff
151	    the token is earned by a premise, by the original question/compiled contract, by a typed renderer
152	    enum, or by pure discourse/orthography. It is NEVER earned by a subject allow-list, because there
153	    is none."""
154	    if token in premise_caps or token in context_caps:
155	        return True
156	    low = token.lower()
157	    if low in premise_blob_lower or _stem(low) in premise_stems:
158	        return True
159	    if context_lower and low in context_lower:
160	        return True
161	    return low in RENDERER_VOCAB or low in _DISCOURSE_CAPS
162	
163	
164	RENDERER_VOCAB = _renderer_vocab()
165	
166	#: BACKWARD-COMPAT ONLY. `SAFE_CAPS` is no longer an authority anywhere in production validation; it is
167	#: retained as the union of the discourse + renderer vocabularies so any external diagnostic that still
168	#: imports the name keeps working. Nothing in `validate()` or `owned_is_safe()` consults it.
169	SAFE_CAPS = {w.capitalize() for w in (_DISCOURSE_CAPS | RENDERER_VOCAB)} | set(_EPISTEMIC_TAGS)
170	
171	
172	# ---- SOL V11 — THE RIDING-FABRICATION GUARD --------------------------------------------------------
173	# A recognised verdict phrase in ONE clause used to license the WHOLE sentence, so a fabrication could
174	# ride a SECOND clause on the same proof. THIS PASSED before the guard and must not:
175	#     'These studies observe different units of analysis, and the intervention eradicates disease.'
176	# The first clause matches _CLAIM_PATTERNS and PROVES as a CONTRASTS_LEVEL verdict; the proof checks the
177	# unit relation and NOTHING proves the eradication rider. A synthesis is a VERDICT ABOUT THE PREMISES,
178	# not a lane to smuggle a first-order finding, so EVERY clause must map to a proof conclusion — a
179	# verdict/relation marker, or a premise-STATED causal mechanism — or introduce NO NEW CONTENT (every
180	# content token already anchored in a premise or discourse). A clause that predicates a fresh, unanchored
181	# token has NO positive proof and is an UNPROVED RIDER -> REJECT. This is a list that may ONLY reject:
182	# an unaccounted content word is UNKNOWN, and the contract fails closed on the unknown (admission requires
183	# positive proof). The owned lane's semantic judge (report_ast) is the second, independent line; this is
184	# the deterministic one, so the moat does not rest on the judge alone.
185	_VERDICT_MARKERS = re.compile('|'.join(re.escape(v) for v in VERDICT_VOCAB), re.I)
186	# Clause boundaries that introduce an INDEPENDENT proposition. Deliberately NOT a bare ' and '/' or '/
187	# comma (those join noun phrases — 'the firm and regional levels', 'creation or reallocation') — only
188	# markers that begin a new predication: ', and', '; and', ';', ', but', ':', 'whereas', 'while',
189	# ', because', and 'and the <NP>' (the exact shape the eradication rider used).
190	_RIDER_SPLIT = re.compile(
191	    r',\s+and\s+|;\s*and\s+|;\s+|,\s+but\s+|:\s+|\s+whereas\s+|\s+while\s+|,\s+because\s+|\band\s+the\s+',
192	    re.I)
193	# Genuine discourse / orthography that may head a clause without being a first-order predicate. Derived
194	# from SAFE_CAPS (so it tracks the same allow-list) plus common function words. It exists ONLY to keep a
195	# pure connective fragment from being read as a rider; it can admit nothing a premise did not already say.
196	_DISCOURSE_WORDS = {w.lower() for w in SAFE_CAPS} | {
197	    'these', 'those', 'this', 'that', 'both', 'either', 'neither', 'taken', 'together', 'however',
198	    'still', 'therefore', 'thus', 'hence', 'while', 'whereas', 'because', 'since', 'although', 'though',
199	    'where', 'when', 'across', 'within', 'among', 'between', 'from', 'into', 'onto', 'than', 'then',
200	    'also', 'only', 'more', 'most', 'less', 'least', 'former', 'latter', 'whether', 'which', 'what',
201	    'does', 'cannot', 'they', 'their', 'them', 'with', 'without', 'about', 'over', 'under', 'and',
202	    'but', 'not', 'are', 'for', 'nor', 'the', 'two', 'one',
203	}
204	
205	
206	def _unproved_rider_clause(s: str, prem: list['Premise']) -> str:
207	    """Every clause of a synthesis must be a proved verdict or introduce no new content; a clause that
208	    predicates a fresh, unanchored token is an UNPROVED RIDER. Returns a refusal reason, or ''."""
209	    blob = ' '.join(p.text + ' ' + p.source for p in prem)
210	    blob_low = blob.lower()
211	    stems = _content_lemmas(blob)
212	    stated = {m.lower() for p in prem for m in p.mechanisms}
213	    for clause in _RIDER_SPLIT.split(s):
214	        low = clause.lower().strip()
215	        if not low:
216	            continue
217	        if _VERDICT_MARKERS.search(low):
218	            continue                                            # a proved verdict/relation clause
219	        if any(re.search(rx, low) for _, _, rx in _CLAIM_PATTERNS):
220	            continue                                            # a recognised verdict pattern
221	        if CAUSAL_IMPORT.search(low) and any(m in low for m in stated):
222	            continue                                            # the already-licensed causal path
223	        for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', low):
224	            if w in _DISCOURSE_WORDS:
225	                continue
226	            if w in blob_low or _stem(w) in stems:
227	                continue                                        # this token is anchored in a premise
228	            return (f'UNPROVED_RIDER_CLAUSE: "{clause.strip()}" asserts "{w}", a predicate no premise '
229	                    f'states and no verdict proves — a synthesis may not smuggle a first-order finding')
230	    return ''
231	
232	
233	@dataclass
234	class Premise:
235	    """An ALREADY-ADMITTED evidence sentence — it passed the existing span-grounding gate.
236	
237	    RUNG 4 adds the facets a PROOF needs. `level` is declared mining metadata and `unit_span` records
238	    whether the short quoted finding also states that unit. A strong unit verdict requires `unit_span`.
239	    The one narrow exception is a CONTRASTS_LEVEL verdict whose closed text explicitly says the unit
240	    labels are declared metadata, not fully span-stated, and calls the comparison indirect. Findings,
241	    outcome and direction remain span-derived; RECONCILES and direction conflicts remain span-bound.
242	    """
243	    id: str
244	    text: str
245	    source: str                      # attribution string, e.g. "Autor et al. (2003), QJE"
246	    level: str = ''                  # task | worker | firm | occupation | region | economy (DECLARED)
247	    horizon: str = ''                # short-run | long-run
248	    method: str = ''                 # experiment | quasi-experimental | observational | survey
249	    mechanisms: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)   # mechanisms STATED IN THE SPAN
250	    # ---- RUNG 4: span-bound facets. Each is derived from THIS premise's verbatim span, or is empty.
251	    outcome: str = ''                # the dependent variable, span-derived
252	    direction: str = ''             # positive | negative | null, span-derived (the SURFACE RESULT)
253	    unit_span: str = ''             # the verbatim excerpt supporting `level`; '' == level not in span
254	    outcome_span: str = ''          # the verbatim excerpt supporting `outcome`
255	    direction_span: str = ''        # the verbatim excerpt supporting `direction`
256	    horizon_span: str = ''          # the verbatim excerpt supporting `horizon`
257	    population: str = ''            # who/where the estimate is about (comparator compatibility)
258	    comparator: str = ''            # against what the effect is measured
259	    modality: str = 'associational'  # associational | causal — a verdict may not upgrade this
260	
261	
262	def _declared_unit_disclosure_failure(text: str, premises: list[Premise]) -> str:
263	    """Return why a declared-only unit contrast is not honestly disclosed, or ''.
264	
265	    Besides the three mandatory disclosures, the parenthetical must reproduce the exact two declared
266	    labels. Thus the exception cannot turn `worker`/`firm` metadata into `region`/`economy` prose.
267	    """
268	    low = text.lower()
269	    required = ('declared metadata', 'not fully stated in the quoted findings', 'indirect comparison')
270	    missing = [x for x in required if x not in low]
271	    if missing:
272	        return f'declared-only unit contrast lacks disclosure: {missing}'
273	    m = re.search(r'different units of analysis \(([^()]*) versus ([^()]*)\)', low)
274	    if not m:
275	        return 'declared-only unit contrast must render its exact labels as "(a versus b)"'
276	    rendered = {m.group(1).strip(), m.group(2).strip()}
277	    declared = {p.level.lower().strip() for p in premises if p.level}
278	    if rendered != declared:
279	        return f'disclosed unit labels {sorted(rendered)} do not match declared labels {sorted(declared)}'
280	    return ''
281

283	@dataclass
284	class Synthesis:
285	    operation: str
286	    premise_ids: list[str]
287	    text: str
288	
289	
290	# ---- LEVEL / UNIT-OF-ANALYSIS SPAN CUES. A declared `level` is only usable in a verdict if one of its
291	#      cues appears VERBATIM in the premise's own span. This is the span-binding of the most dangerous
292	#      facet in a reconciliation: the axis it turns on. (argument_planner.py:599 read `level` straight off
293	#      the card; a card can declare `firm` over a span that only ever says `regions`.)
294	LEVEL_CUES: dict[str, list[str]] = {
295	    'task':       ['task'],
296	    'worker':     ['worker', 'individual', 'employee', 'labor market', 'labour market'],
297	    'occupation': ['occupation', 'job'],
298	    # SOL V11: 'plant' removed — it is POLYSEMOUS (factory vs botanical) and would span-support a declared
299	    # 'firm' level off a sentence about vegetation. A cue that a verdict axis turns on must be unambiguous;
300	    # an ambiguous token is UNKNOWN, so it is dropped (level_span_support then returns '' and the verdict
301	    # fails closed) rather than trusted. Cues left here are firm-of-organisation senses only.
302	    'firm':       ['firm', 'establishment', 'company', 'employer', 'business'],
303	    'industry':   ['industry', 'industries', 'industrial', 'sector'],
304	    'region':     ['region', 'local', 'commuting zone', 'area', 'city', 'county'],
305	    'economy':    ['econom', 'aggregate', 'national', 'country', 'macro', 'nationwide'],
306	}
307	
308	
309	def level_span_support(span: str, level: str) -> str:
310	    """The verbatim excerpt of `span` that supports the declared `level`, or '' if the span never states
311	    it. A declared level with no span support MAY NOT be a verdict axis (RUNG 4, obligation 2)."""
312	    if not span or not level:
313	        return ''
314	    low = span.lower()
315	    for cue in LEVEL_CUES.get(level.lower(), [level.lower()]):
316	        i = low.find(cue)
317	        if i >= 0:
318	            # widen to the surrounding word so the audit trail is a real token, not a fragment
319	            j, k = i, i + len(cue)
320	            while j > 0 and (low[j - 1].isalpha() or low[j - 1] in "-"):
321	                j -= 1
322	            while k < len(low) and (low[k].isalpha() or low[k] in "-"):
323	                k += 1
324	            return span[j:k]
325	    return ''
326	
327	
328	def _stem(w: str) -> str:
329	    """Crude stemmer. Needed because 'task level' must anchor to a premise that says 'tasks',
330	    and 'effect' to one that says 'effects'. Anchoring is a RELEVANCE check, not a safety check —
331	    loosening it cannot admit a fabrication (the entity/number/mechanism gates do that work)."""
332	    for suf in ('ies', 'es', 's'):
333	        if w.endswith(suf) and len(w) - len(suf) >= 4:
334	            return w[: -len(suf)]
335	    return w
336	
337	
338	def _content_lemmas(s: str) -> set[str]:
339	    return {_stem(w) for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', s.lower())}
340	
341	
342	def validate(syn: Synthesis, premises: dict[str, Premise], context: str = '') -> tuple[bool, str]:
343	    """Deterministic. No LLM. Returns (ok, reason_if_rejected).
344	
345	    `context` is the original question / compiled-contract text (optional). A capitalised token that
346	    appears in it is EARNED — the reviewer asked about it — exactly as a token appearing in a premise
347	    is. This is what lets a clinical, a legal, or a CS review name its OWN subject without any per-domain
348	    allow-list: the subject is in the question, so it is not a fabricated entity."""
349	    s = syn.text.strip()
350	    if not s:
351	        return False, 'empty'
352	
353	    # 1. the operation must be one we defined
354	    if syn.operation not in OPERATIONS:
355	        return False, f'unknown_operation:{syn.operation}'
356	
357	    # 2. premises must exist and be ADMITTED evidence
358	    prem = [premises[p] for p in syn.premise_ids if p in premises]
359	    if len(prem) != len(syn.premise_ids):
360	        missing = [p for p in syn.premise_ids if p not in premises]
361	        return False, f'premise_not_admitted:{missing}'
362	
363	    # 3. relational operations need >= 2 premises from >= 2 DISTINCT sources
364	    relational = syn.operation not in ('COVERAGE_GAP',)
365	    if relational:
366	        if len(prem) < 2:
367	            return False, 'relational_operation_needs_2+_premises'
368	        if len({p.source for p in prem}) < 2:
369	            return False, 'premises_share_a_single_source'
370	
371	    # 4. NO NEW NUMBER. Ever. (A number belongs to an EVIDENCE sentence, not a synthesis.)
372	    if NUMERIC.search(s):
373	        return False, 'synthesis_carries_a_digit'
374	    if SPELLED_QTY.search(s):
375	        return False, f'synthesis_carries_a_spelled_quantity:{SPELLED_QTY.search(s).group(0)}'
376	
377	    # 5. NO NEW ENTITY. Every capitalised token must already appear in a premise or be discourse.
378	    #    Hyphenated compounds are checked COMPONENT-WISE ("Firm-level" -> "Firm", "level"), because a
379	    #    compound built from known words introduces no new entity. Each component still has to clear
380	    #    the same bar, so this cannot let a fabricated name through.
381	    blob = ' '.join(p.text + ' ' + p.source for p in prem)
382	    blob_lower = blob.lower()
383	    prem_caps = set(CAP_TOKEN.findall(blob))
384	    prem_stems = _content_lemmas(blob)
385	    ctx_lower = (context or '').lower()
386	    ctx_caps = set(CAP_TOKEN.findall(context or ''))
387	    # A PROPER NOUN is a capitalised token appearing MID-SENTENCE. A capital at the start of a sentence
388	    # (or after a full stop) is ORTHOGRAPHY, not an entity. Turn 2 rejected "Whether", "Reconciliation",
389	    # "AI's" and "driven" (from "AI-driven") as FABRICATED ENTITIES — every one a false positive, and
390	    # together they deleted 74 legitimate sentences. The gate's job is to catch INVENTED NAMES, not
391	    # capital letters.
392	    sentence_initial = set()
393	    for m in re.finditer(r'(?:^|[.!?]\s+)([A-Z][A-Za-z&.\-\']*)', s):
394	        sentence_initial.add(m.group(1))
395	    for tok in CAP_TOKEN.findall(s):
396	        if tok in sentence_initial:
397	            continue                      # orthography, not an entity
398	        tok = re.sub(r"'s$", '', tok)     # strip the possessive: "AI's" -> "AI"
399	        for part in re.split(r'[-–]', tok):
400	            if not part:
401	                continue
402	            # THE ONE STRUCTURAL PREDICATE (Sol P1): earned by a premise, the question/contract, a typed
403	            # renderer enum, or pure discourse — never by a subject allow-list. Same rule owned_is_safe runs.
404	            if caps_earned(part, premise_caps=prem_caps, premise_blob_lower=blob_lower,
405	                           premise_stems=prem_stems, context_lower=ctx_lower, context_caps=ctx_caps):
406	                continue
407	            return False, f'new_entity:{part}'
408	
409	    # 6. NO IMPORTED MECHANISM, AND NO FABRICATED BINDING.
410	    #    A fabrication can be assembled ENTIRELY FROM TRUE PARTICULARS: bind a REAL mechanism to a REAL
411	    #    paper that never states it. Found live on disk: "task displacement" (Autor-Levy-Murnane's term)
412	    #    attributed to Bresnahan et al. (2002), whose span never says it. No "new entity" rule catches
413	    #    that -- both the mechanism and the paper are real. THE LIE IS IN THE BINDING.
414	    #    So we check TWO things: (a) is a causal mechanism asserted at all, and (b) if the sentence NAMES
415	    #    a source, does THAT source's own span state the mechanism it is being credited with?
416	    m = CAUSAL_IMPORT.search(s)
417	    if m:
418	        stated = {mm.lower() for p in prem for mm in p.mechanisms}
419	        if not stated:
420	            return False, f'causal_language_with_no_stated_mechanism:"{m.group(0)}"'
421	        if not any(mech in s.lower() for mech in stated):
422	            return False, f'causal_language_names_a_mechanism_no_premise_states:"{m.group(0)}"'
423	    # (b) THE BINDING CHECK -- runs whether or not causal vocabulary is present.
424	    #     If the sentence names an author from a premise AND asserts a mechanism, that mechanism must be
425	    #     stated by THAT author's premise -- not by some other paper in the room.
426	    for p in prem:
427	        surname = p.source.split()[0].rstrip(',')
428	        if len(surname) < 4 or surname.lower() not in s.lower():
429	            continue                      # this source is not named in the sentence
430	        for other in prem:
431	            if other is p:
432	                continue
433	            for mech in other.mechanisms:
434	                if mech.lower() in s.lower() and mech.lower() not in {x.lower() for x in p.mechanisms}:
435	                    return False, (f'FABRICATED_BINDING: "{mech}" is stated by {other.source.split()[0]}, '
436	                                   f'not by {surname} — the sentence credits the wrong paper')
437	
438	    # 7. NO FORECAST, NO UNIVERSAL
439	    if FORECAST.search(s):
440	        return False, f'forecast_or_prediction:"{FORECAST.search(s).group(0)}"'
441	    if UNIVERSAL.search(s):
442	        return False, f'universal_overclaim:"{UNIVERSAL.search(s).group(0)}"'
443	
444	    # 8. (REMOVED AS A HARD GATE.) This demanded "verdict vocabulary" of every uncited sentence and
445	    #    DELETED 163 sentences in wheel turn 1 — gutting the report to 4,021 words, below the length
446	    #    floor. It is a STYLE RULE WEARING A SAFETY RULE'S CLOTHES: a sentence that reads as a "vibe"
447	    #    is weak prose, not a fabrication. Adjudication is what the RUBRIC pays for; the CONTRACT's job
448	    #    is only to make lying impossible. Conflating the two is why our gate rejects 97% of the prose
449	    #    that the judge scores 9.8/10. Style belongs in the writer's PROMPT, not in the safety gate.
450	
451	    # 9. operation-specific structural obligations, checked against DECLARED fields only
452	    if syn.operation == 'CONTRASTS_LEVEL':
453	        if len({p.level for p in prem if p.level}) < 2:
454	            return False, 'CONTRASTS_LEVEL but the premises do not declare 2 distinct levels'
455	    if syn.operation == 'CONTRASTS_HORIZON':
456	        if len({p.horizon for p in prem if p.horizon}) < 2:
457	            return False, 'CONTRASTS_HORIZON but the premises do not declare 2 distinct horizons'
458	    if syn.operation == 'CONTRASTS_METHOD' or syn.operation == 'RANK_EVIDENCE':
459	        if len({p.method for p in prem if p.method}) < 2:
460	            return False, f'{syn.operation} but the premises do not declare 2 distinct methods'
461	
462	    # 10. must be anchored in the premises it claims to relate. A declared-only unit contrast can have
463	    #     exactly one shared span lemma (the outcome), because by definition neither unit need occur in
464	    #     the quotes. The one-lemma threshold is confined to the exact-label disclosed template.
465	    disclosed_indirect = (syn.operation == 'CONTRASTS_LEVEL'
466	                          and any(not p.unit_span for p in prem)
467	                          and not _declared_unit_disclosure_failure(s, prem))
468	    min_anchors = 1 if disclosed_indirect else 2
469	    if len(_content_lemmas(s) & _content_lemmas(blob)) < min_anchors:
470	        return False, 'not_anchored_in_its_premises'
471	
472	    # 11. SOL V11 — NO RIDING FABRICATION. Steps 1-10 prove the RELATION the sentence's recognised phrase
473	    #     asserts; they never inspect the REST of the sentence. A recognised verdict clause may not license
474	    #     an unproved rider ('...observe different units, and the intervention eradicates disease'). Every
475	    #     clause must be a proved verdict or introduce no new content, else the sentence smuggles a finding.
476	    why_rider = _unproved_rider_clause(s, prem)
477	    if why_rider:
478	        return False, why_rider
479	
480	    return True, ''
481	
482	
483	# =================================================================================================
484	# RUNG 4 — PROOF-CARRYING VERDICTS. `validate()` above proves a verdict is SAFE (no new particular,
485	# anchored, no imported mechanism). It does NOT prove the verdict is TRUE: it let CONVERGES, CONTRASTS,
486	# ESTABLISHES and DOES_NOT_ESTABLISH pass on anchoring alone, so the gate admitted BOTH "these point in
487	# opposite directions" AND "these are not contradictory" for the SAME premises — a false reconciliation
488	# assembled entirely from true particulars. A verdict now needs a PROOF OBJECT, not a tag: an operation
489	# whose preconditions are checked against SPAN-BOUND facets, with the exact excerpt that supports each.
490	# =================================================================================================
491	
492	@dataclass
493	class RelationProof:
494	    """The receipt a verdict must carry. Span-derived dimensions retain their verbatim support. For the
495	    narrow disclosed-indirect CONTRASTS_LEVEL case, an empty unit excerpt honestly records that the unit
496	    came only from declared mining metadata; every other relational use of unit remains span-bound."""
497	    operation: str
498	    claim_class: str
499	    premise_ids: list[str]
500	    shared: dict[str, list[tuple[str, str]]] = field(default_factory=dict)     # dim -> [(pid, span)]
501	    differing: dict[str, list[tuple[str, str]]] = field(default_factory=dict)  # dim -> [(pid, value, span)]
502	    polarity: dict[str, str] = field(default_factory=dict)                     # pid -> direction
503	    modality: dict[str, str] = field(default_factory=dict)                     # pid -> associational|causal
504	    comparator: dict[str, str] = field(default_factory=dict)                   # pid -> comparator
505	    rule: str = ''                                                             # the precondition satisfied
506	    conclusion: str = ''                                                       # the licensed claim template
507	
508	
509	# ---- THE VERDICT CLASS A SENTENCE ASSERTS. A verdict is checked against the proof of the operation that
510	#      licenses ITS OWN claim, not against "any operation that happens to pass". Order is load-bearing:
511	#      a reconciliation ("not contradictory") is tested before the milder "different units" reading,
512	#      because a sentence that says both must be held to the stronger obligation.
513	_CLAIM_PATTERNS: list[tuple[str, str, str]] = [
514	    # (claim_class, licensing_operation, regex)
515	    ('RECONCILES', 'RECONCILES',
516	     r'not contradictory|are consistent with|do not (?:conflict|contradict)|only look contradictory|'
517	     r'can both be true|simultaneously true|reconcil'),
518	    ('CONTRASTS_DIRECTION', 'CONTRASTS_DIRECTION',
519	     r'opposite direction|genuinely conflict|cannot be dissolved|does not speak with one voice|'
520	     r'\bin tension\b|point in opposite|remains unresolved|sets against itself|genuinely disagree'),
521	    ('CONTRASTS_LEVEL', 'CONTRASTS_LEVEL',
522	     r'different units? of analysis|not directly comparable|different levels?|'
523	     r'what holds at .* (?:does not|not) .*establish|does not establish .* at the .* level|'
524	     r'observe different units'),
525	    ('CONTRASTS_HORIZON', 'CONTRASTS_HORIZON',
526	     r'different time horizon|transitional effect|settled one|observe different .* horizon'),
527	    ('RANK_EVIDENCE', 'RANK_EVIDENCE',
528	     r'rests on (?:a|an) \w+ design|more securely|stronger design|'
529	     r'different identification (?:strategy|design)'),
530	    ('CONVERGES', 'CONVERGES',
531	     r'\bconverg|point in the same direction|same directional finding|agree on'),
532	    # A PURE LIMITATION STATEMENT asserts that the evidence CANNOT do something. It reconciles nothing and
533	    # relates no two directions, so it cannot be a false reconciliation; it is licensed by anchoring alone.
534	    ('BOUNDARY', 'BOUNDARY',
535	     r'does not settle|not on the same footing|cannot distinguish|is limited to|rests on a single '
536	     r'source|no other study|does not extend beyond|cannot establish'),
537	]
538	
539	
540	def classify_claim(text: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
541	    """(claim_class, licensing_operation). '' if the sentence makes no recognised verdict — in which case
542	    the gate FAILS CLOSED: an owned synthesis whose claim cannot be identified cannot be proved."""
543	    low = text.lower()
544	    for claim_class, op, rx in _CLAIM_PATTERNS:
545	        if re.search(rx, low):
546	            return claim_class, op
547	    return '', ''
548	
549	
550	def _both_known_opposed(a: Premise, b: Premise) -> bool:
551	    return (bool(a.direction) and bool(b.direction)
552	            and {a.direction, b.direction} in ({'positive', 'negative'},
553	                                               {'positive', 'null'}, {'negative', 'null'}))
554	
555	
556	def prove(operation: str, premises: list[Premise], text: str) -> tuple[RelationProof | None, str]:
557	    """Build the proof for `operation` over `premises`, or refuse with a reason. This is the whole point
558	    of RUNG 4: each operation verifies ITS OWN preconditions against span-bound facets. A verdict ships
559	    ONLY if the proof for the operation THAT LICENSES ITS CLAIM is issued. There is no "try them all".
560	    """
561	    if operation not in OPERATIONS:
562	        return None, f'unknown_operation:{operation}'
563	    # BOUNDARY / COVERAGE_GAP are NON-RELATIONAL: they state a limit of the evidence and relate no two
564	    # findings, so they neither need a second premise nor a second source. Every other operation does.
565	    non_relational = operation in ('BOUNDARY', 'COVERAGE_GAP')
566	    if not non_relational:
567	        if len(premises) < 2:
568	            return None, 'a relational verdict needs at least two premises'
569	        if len({p.source for p in premises if p.source}) < 2:
570	            return None, 'the premises share a single source — a paper cannot corroborate itself'
571	
572	    a = premises[0]
573	    b = premises[1] if len(premises) > 1 else premises[0]
574	    ids = [p.id for p in premises]
575	
576	    # SAME CONSTRUCT. Almost every operation first requires the premises to be about the SAME outcome;
577	    # opposed results on DIFFERENT outcomes are not in tension and cannot be reconciled or contrasted.
578	    outcomes = {p.outcome for p in premises if p.outcome}
579	    shared_outcome = bool(outcomes) and len(outcomes) == 1 and all(p.outcome for p in premises)
580	
581	    def _unit_spans_present() -> str:
582	        for p in premises:
583	            if p.level and not p.unit_span:
584	                return (f'the {p.id} card declares level "{p.level}" but no verbatim span supports it — '
585	                        f'a facet a verdict turns on must be bound to the span')
586	            if not p.level:
587	                return f'the {p.id} card declares no unit of analysis'
588	        return ''
589	
590	    def _proof(claim_class, shared, differing, rule, conclusion) -> tuple[RelationProof, str]:
591	        return RelationProof(
592	            operation=operation, claim_class=claim_class, premise_ids=ids,
593	            shared=shared, differing=differing,
594	            polarity={p.id: p.direction for p in premises},
595	            modality={p.id: p.modality for p in premises},
596	            comparator={p.id: p.comparator for p in premises},
597	            rule=rule, conclusion=conclusion), ''
598	
599	    # ---- RECONCILES: "these opposed results are NOT contradictory". The strongest claim on the board,
600	    #      and the one the adversary forged. It is licensed ONLY by a full reconciliation: same construct,
601	    #      opposed surface results, a scope (unit) that DIFFERS and is span-bound on both premises, and a
602	    #      compatible time base. Same scope + opposed results is a GENUINE conflict, not a reconciliation.
603	    if operation == 'RECONCILES':
604	        if not shared_outcome:
605	            return None, 'reconciliation requires the same construct on both premises (no shared outcome)'
606	        if not _both_known_opposed(a, b):
607	            return None, ('reconciliation requires OPPOSED, span-derived surface results — there is '
608	                          'nothing to reconcile unless the two findings actually oppose')
609	        why = _unit_spans_present()
610	        if why:
611	            return None, why
612	        if len({p.level for p in premises}) < 2:
613	            return None, ('the premises share a scope, so opposed results are a GENUINE CONFLICT, not a '
614	                          'reconciliation — there is no scope difference that lets both be true at once')
615	        hs = {p.horizon for p in premises if p.horizon}
616	        if len(hs) > 1:
617	            return None, ('the premises also differ in time horizon, so the difference is not cleanly '
618	                          'attributable to scope — the reconciliation is unproved')
619	        return _proof(
620	            'RECONCILES',
621	            shared={'outcome': [(p.id, p.outcome_span or p.outcome) for p in premises]},
622	            differing={'unit_of_analysis': [(p.id, p.level, p.unit_span) for p in premises]},
623	            rule='same construct + opposed span-derived results + span-bound DIFFERING scope + compatible '
624	                 'horizon => both can be simultaneously true',
625	            conclusion='not_contradictory: they observe different units of analysis')
626	
627	    # ---- CONTRASTS_DIRECTION: "the evidence genuinely conflicts / points in opposite directions". A REAL
628	    #      conflict: same construct, SAME span-bound scope, opposed span-derived directions. The scope
629	    #      must be the SAME — opposed results at DIFFERENT units are reconcilable, not a conflict.
630	    if operation == 'CONTRASTS_DIRECTION':
631	        if not shared_outcome:
632	            return None, 'a direction conflict requires the same construct (no shared outcome)'
633	        if not _both_known_opposed(a, b):
634	            return None, 'the directions are not both span-derived and opposed'
635	        why = _unit_spans_present()
636	        if why:
637	            return None, why
638	        if len({p.level for p in premises}) > 1:
639	            return None, ('the premises observe different units, so opposed results are not a direct '
640	                          'conflict — they are reconcilable by scope, not "genuinely contradictory"')
641	        return _proof(
642	            'CONTRASTS_DIRECTION',
643	            shared={'outcome': [(p.id, p.outcome_span or p.outcome) for p in premises],
644	                    'unit_of_analysis': [(p.id, p.unit_span) for p in premises]},
645	            differing={'direction': [(p.id, p.direction, p.direction_span or p.direction) for p in premises]},
646	            rule='same construct + SAME span-bound scope + opposed span-derived directions => genuine '
647	                 'conflict that cannot be dissolved by appeal to level',
648	            conclusion='genuine_conflict: opposed at the same unit of analysis')
649	
650	    # ---- CONTRASTS_LEVEL: the MILD reading — "these concern different units and are not directly
651	    #      comparable". Strong when both units are span-bound; otherwise admitted ONLY when the closed
652	    #      verdict discloses declared metadata + missing quote support + indirectness. It never licenses
653	    #      "not contradictory" (RECONCILES remains fully span-bound above).
654	    if operation == 'CONTRASTS_LEVEL':
655	        if not shared_outcome:
656	            return None, 'a unit contrast requires the same construct (no shared outcome)'
657	        why = _unit_spans_present()
658	        if why:
659	            disclosure_failure = _declared_unit_disclosure_failure(text, premises)
660	            if disclosure_failure:
661	                return None, f'{why}; {disclosure_failure}'
662	        if len({p.level for p in premises}) < 2:
663	            return None, 'the premises do not declare two distinct units of analysis'
664	        declared_only = any(not p.unit_span for p in premises)
665	        return _proof(
666	            'CONTRASTS_LEVEL',
667	            shared={'outcome': [(p.id, p.outcome_span or p.outcome) for p in premises]},
668	            differing={'unit_of_analysis': [(p.id, p.level, p.unit_span) for p in premises]},
669	            rule=('same span-derived construct + DIFFERING declared scope + explicit metadata/non-span/'
670	                  'indirect disclosure => indirect comparison'
671	                  if declared_only else
672	                  'same construct + span-bound DIFFERING scope => not directly comparable'),
673	            conclusion=('declared_different_units_indirect_comparison' if declared_only else
674	                        'different_units_not_comparable'))
675	
676	    # ---- CONTRASTS_HORIZON: same construct, differing declared time horizon.
677	    if operation == 'CONTRASTS_HORIZON':
678	        if not shared_outcome:
679	            return None, 'a horizon contrast requires the same construct (no shared outcome)'
680	        hs = {p.horizon for p in premises if p.horizon}
681	        if len([p for p in premises if p.horizon]) < len(premises):
682	            return None, 'at least one premise declares no horizon'
683	        if len(hs) < 2:
684	            return None, 'the premises do not declare two distinct horizons'
685	        return _proof(
686	            'CONTRASTS_HORIZON',
687	            shared={'outcome': [(p.id, p.outcome_span or p.outcome) for p in premises]},
688	            differing={'horizon': [(p.id, p.horizon, p.horizon_span or p.horizon) for p in premises]},
689	            rule='same construct + differing horizon => a horizon spread',
690	            conclusion='different_horizons')
691	
692	    # ---- RANK_EVIDENCE: same directional finding, differing declared method.
693	    if operation == 'RANK_EVIDENCE':
694	        ms = {p.method for p in premises if p.method}
695	        if len([p for p in premises if p.method]) < len(premises):
696	            return None, 'at least one premise declares no method'
697	        if len(ms) < 2:
698	            return None, 'the premises do not declare two distinct methods to rank'
699	        return _proof(
700	            'RANK_EVIDENCE',
701	            shared={'outcome': [(p.id, p.outcome_span or p.outcome) for p in premises]},
702	            differing={'method': [(p.id, p.method, p.method) for p in premises]},
703	            rule='same finding + differing method => the stronger design ranks higher',
704	            conclusion='ranked_by_design')
705	
706	    # ---- CONVERGES: the premises AGREE in span-derived direction on the same construct.
707	    if operation == 'CONVERGES':
708	        if not shared_outcome:
709	            return None, 'convergence requires the same construct (no shared outcome)'
710	        dirs = {p.direction for p in premises if p.direction}
711	        if len([p for p in premises if p.direction]) < len(premises):
712	            return None, 'convergence requires a span-derived direction on every premise'
713	        if len(dirs) != 1:
714	            return None, 'the premises do not point in the same direction — this is not convergence'
715	        return _proof(
716	            'CONVERGES',
717	            shared={'outcome': [(p.id, p.outcome_span or p.outcome) for p in premises],
718	                    'direction': [(p.id, p.direction, p.direction_span or p.direction) for p in premises]},
719	            differing={},
720	            rule='same construct + same span-derived direction => convergence',
721	            conclusion='converges')
722	
723	    # ---- BOUNDARY / COVERAGE_GAP: a pure limitation or a derivable gap. They relate no two directions
724	    #      and reconcile nothing, so the only obligation is that the premises are admitted — they cannot
725	    #      be a false verdict.
726	    if operation in ('BOUNDARY', 'COVERAGE_GAP'):
727	        return _proof(operation, shared={}, differing={},
728	                      rule='a limitation/gap statement asserts no relation, so it carries no reconciliation',
729	                      conclusion='states a limit of the evidence')
730	
731	    return None, f'no proof rule for operation {operation}'
732	
733	
734	def prove_owned(text: str, premises: list[Premise]) -> tuple[RelationProof | None, str]:
735	    """The release-boundary entry point. Classify the CLAIM the sentence makes, then demand the proof of
736	    the operation that licenses THAT claim. Fails closed on an unclassifiable claim."""
737	    claim_class, op = classify_claim(text)
738	    if not op:
739	        return None, ('the sentence makes no recognised verdict — an owned synthesis whose claim cannot '
740	                      'be identified cannot be proved, so it is refused')
741	    proof, why = prove(op, premises, text)
742	    if proof is None:

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""THE TYPED REPORT AST — THE LAW, MECHANISED. There is no other way to say a sentence.
3	
4	    Every sentence is ATTRIBUTED (names a source -> MUST be entailed by THAT source's VERBATIM SPAN)
5	    or OWNED (reviewer's voice -> names no source, carries no new particular, MAY be non-entailed).
6	    THE VERBATIM SPAN IS THE ONLY EVIDENCE. The model-written `claim` is a display cache; nothing is
7	    ever validated against it.
8	
9	WHY THIS FILE EXISTS, AND WHY IT IS NOT ANOTHER PREFLIGHT
10	---------------------------------------------------------
11	Six adversary attacks succeeded while `test_gate_is_wired.py` was 16/16 GREEN and nothing had been
12	weakened. The reason was not a weak check. It was that **the checks certified a lane the fabrication no
13	longer used**: 6,463 lines of correct, self-tested modules were dead code, and the composer imported not
14	one of them. `provenance.py` passed its own 18/18 self-test while the P0 it was built to stop sat live on
15	disk.
16	
17	So the gate is no longer a function the writer is asked to call. IT IS THE ONLY TYPE A SENTENCE CAN HAVE.
18	A sentence that has not been through here is not "ungated" — IT DOES NOT EXIST, because prose is never
19	concatenated anywhere in this pipeline. `render()` is the only thing that emits characters, it emits them
20	only from validated nodes, and `publisher.py` is the only thing that may write them into the release
21	directory.
22	
23	WHAT CHANGED IN THE SHAPE OF A SENTENCE
24	---------------------------------------
25	The old composer inferred WHO A SENTENCE WAS ABOUT BY LOOKING FOR SURNAMES IN IT (`_cited_cards`). That
26	is how "Bresnahan reports task displacement" — a real mechanism, a real paper, a FABRICATED BINDING —
27	was gated against whichever card the regex happened to hit first. Sol: **DO NOT INFER VOICE OR SOURCE
28	IDENTITY FROM SURNAMES.**
29	
30	Here, the model does not write attribution prose AT ALL, and it does not get to imply a source by naming
31	one. It emits a bare FINDING plus THE CARD ID it came from:
32	
33	    Attributed(clauses=[Clause(card_id='...:76927-77299', text='the employment-to-population ratio
34	                               falls by 0.37 percentage points per robot per thousand workers')])
35	
36	and THIS FILE renders the attribution — from the graph, from the expression the SOURCE POLICY selected.
37	The model cannot copy an attribution, cannot invent one, and cannot name a journal whose bytes we do not
38	hold, because it never types the journal's name.
39	
40	A CROSS-SOURCE SENTENCE IS A CONJUNCTION OF ATTRIBUTED CLAUSES, EACH BOUND TO ITS OWN CARD. That keeps
41	the heaviest criterion on the board (critical synthesis, w=0.0800) reachable — the old gate deleted every
42	comparative sentence — while the fabricated binding still dies, because the clause that says "task
43	displacement" names Bresnahan's card_id and 'task displacement' is not in Bresnahan's span.
44	
45	THE CHAIN, WHICH IS THE WHOLE POINT
46	-----------------------------------
47	    sentence -> card -> bound span -> manifestation_id + content_hash -> permitted expression -> attribution
48	
49	`CardBundle.resolve()` IS that chain, as one function, and it is the only door. Nothing in this file, in
50	`cellcog_composer.py`, or in `publisher.py` reads a card's `attribution`, `venue`, `doi` or `authors` to
51	decide anything. Those are display caches. The graph decides.
52	"""
53	from __future__ import annotations
54	
55	import hashlib
56	import html
57	import os
58	import re
59	import sys
60	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
61	from pathlib import Path
62	
63	ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
64	sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT))
65	sys.path.insert(0, str(ROOT / 'scripts'))
66	
67	import provenance as P  # noqa: E402
68	from synthesis_contract import (Premise, Synthesis, validate,  # noqa: E402
69	                                SPELLED_QTY, FORECAST, classify_claim, prove,
70	                                level_span_support)
71	
72	
73	# =================================================================================================
74	# 0. THE NODES. A report is a list of these and NOTHING ELSE.
75	# =================================================================================================
76	
77	@dataclass(frozen=True)
78	class Clause:
79	    """ONE finding, from ONE card. The text is the FINDING ONLY — no attribution, no journal, no year.
80	
81	    `card_id` is not a hint and it is not decoration: it is the sentence's ONLY connection to reality,
82	    and it is checked against the bytes. The model may not name a source in `text`; if it does, the
83	    clause is refused (SOURCE_NAMED_IN_CLAUSE_TEXT), because a named source in model prose is a source
84	    WE DID NOT RESOLVE.
85	    """
86	    card_id: str
87	    text: str
88	
89	
90	#: How clause i>0 joins the clause before it. A CLOSED SET — the model may not write connective prose,
91	#: because a connective is where an unsourced particular hides ("..., which explains why 40% of...").
92	#:
93	#: The set is SPLIT by what the connective ASSERTS. A NEUTRAL joiner asserts only conjunction or
94	#: simultaneity — "X finds a, while/and Y finds b" — a relation both spans already license by merely
95	#: co-existing. A CONTRASTIVE joiner asserts that the second finding OPPOSES the first ("whereas",
96	#: "by contrast", "but", "yet", "though"); THAT relation is a claim, and it is a claim NEITHER
97	#: span-check tests — the lie sits BETWEEN the clauses, outside both. Sol admitted two POSITIVE,
98	#: unrelated findings joined by "by contrast"; neither source entails the contrast. Until a
99	#: proof-carrying planner verdict supplies the relation (an Owned synthesis, rung 4), the WRITER may
100	#: not assert it, so a model-chosen contrastive connective is REFUSED. `while` is retained as a
101	#: neutral simultaneity joiner: it asserts no direction of opposition, and the honest cross-source
102	#: synthesis — the most valuable sentence in the review — is written with it.
103	NEUTRAL_CONNECTIVES = ('while', 'and')
104	CONTRASTIVE_CONNECTIVES = ('whereas', 'by contrast', 'but', 'yet', 'though')
105	CONNECTIVES = NEUTRAL_CONNECTIVES + CONTRASTIVE_CONNECTIVES

110	    """A sentence that names sources. EVERY clause is entailed by ITS OWN card's verbatim span."""
111	    clauses: tuple[Clause, ...]
112	    connective: str = 'while'

115	        return tuple(c.card_id for c in self.clauses)

118	@dataclass(frozen=True)
119	class Quotation:
120	    """THE QUOTATION LANE (SOL V11 §1a). The ONLY deterministic ADMIT in this file.
121	
122	    The payload MUST be BYTE-IDENTICAL to the WHOLE bound evidence unit — case, punctuation, signs,
123	    quote characters and internal whitespace ALL preserved, with NO normalization, because case and
124	    punctuation are semantic (gene symbols, math, question marks). It is rendered NEUTRALLY —
125	    `<Author> writes: "<exact bytes>"` — and NEVER as show/find/establish/'the study found'. Its ONLY
126	    guarantee is that THESE BYTES OCCUR IN THIS SOURCE; it makes no claim that the source FOUND or
127	    ESTABLISHED anything, so a quoted HYPOTHESIS or QUESTION is reproduced faithfully AS a quotation and
128	    is never upgraded into a finding. A payload that is a window, a paraphrase, or a normalized form of
129	    the span is NOT a quotation: it must go through the finding lane and the judge.
130	    """
131	    card_id: str
132	    text: str
133	
134	
135	@dataclass(frozen=True)
136	class Owned:
137	    """The reviewer's voice. MAY BE NON-ENTAILED — that is what insight IS.
138	
139	    It must name NO source and carry NO new particular. Two regimes, one type:
140	
141	      * `premise_ids` non-empty  -> a SYNTHESIS over admitted premises. It must additionally pass a
142	        typed operation in `synthesis_contract` (a mechanism may appear ONLY if a premise states it).
143	        This is the body lane.
144	      * `premise_ids` empty      -> a FRAME sentence (an abstract's "Objective.", a section's bridge).
145	        It is licensed by nothing and may therefore assert nothing particular: no number, no source, no
146	        proper noun the review has not already earned. This is the lane the hand-written abstract used
147	        to bypass the law through, and it is now a TYPE with a CHECK, not an f-string in write_report().
148	    """
149	    text: str
150	    premise_ids: tuple[str, ...] = ()
151	
152	
153	@dataclass(frozen=True)
154	class ProvenVerdict(Owned):
155	    """A DETERMINISTIC, PROOF-CARRYING verdict — the reviewer's adjudication over admitted premises,
156	    RENDERED BY THE PLANNER FROM A CLOSED CANONICAL TEMPLATE, never by a model.
157	
158	    The ordinary `Owned` synthesis lane depends, at its final step, on a GLM frame-classification call
159	    (`_owned_frame_empirical`) — a second live-model dependency that can silently drop a verdict the
160	    proof already licensed, and was one of the paths to `0 verdicts placed`. A `ProvenVerdict` removes
161	    THAT redundancy and NOTHING ELSE: its text is one of our own closed templates whose claim class the
162	    planner rendered, so after validation has RE-RESOLVED every premise, RE-DERIVED its span facets,
163	    RE-RUN `validate()`+`prove()`, and confirmed the declared `operation` is exactly the one the text's
164	    claim licenses, the smuggle judge is skipped. Everything else the Owned lane checks still runs.
165	
166	    IT IS FORGE-RESISTANT BY CONSTRUCTION: the LLM-JSON parser builds only plain `Owned` nodes, so a
167	    model can never emit this type; only the deterministic factory `make_proven_verdict()` constructs it.
168	    A frozen dataclass cannot be mutated after construction, so `operation` and `text` cannot drift apart
169	    once proved. A forged instance, a stale/mismatched operation, an unknown operation, or a same-source
170	    pair still rejects through the SAME proof pipeline. A declared-only unit is admitted solely for the
171	    closed, disclosed-indirect CONTRASTS_LEVEL template; strong reconciliation remains span-bound.
172	    """
173	    operation: str = ""
174	
175	
176	def proven_verdict_or_failures(text: str, premise_ids, b: 'CardBundle'):
177	    """THE ONLY DOOR to a `ProvenVerdict`, returning `(node_or_None, failures)` so a caller can bucket
178	    WHY a candidate did not place (the composer's refusal histogram). The deterministic planner hands us
179	    a canonical verdict string; we classify the operation its claim licenses, build the frozen
180	    proof-carrying node, and RE-GATE it through the full validator. Fewer than two premises, an
181	    unclassifiable claim, or any revalidation failure yields `(None, [Failure, ...])`."""
182	    if len(premise_ids) < 2:
183	        return None, [Failure(0, 'ProvenVerdict', 'PROVEN_VERDICT_NEEDS_2_PREMISES', str(premise_ids))]
184	    from synthesis_contract import classify_claim as _cc  # noqa: PLC0415
185	    t = (text or '').strip()
186	    if not t:
187	        return None, [Failure(0, 'ProvenVerdict', 'EMPTY_VERDICT_TEXT', '')]
188	    _claim, op = _cc(t)
189	    if not op:
190	        return None, [Failure(0, 'ProvenVerdict', 'OWNED_VERDICT_UNCLASSIFIABLE', t[:60])]
191	    node = ProvenVerdict(text=t, premise_ids=tuple(premise_ids), operation=op)
192	    fails = validate_report([node], b)
193	    return (node if not fails else None), fails

196	def make_proven_verdict(text: str, premise_ids, b: 'CardBundle') -> 'ProvenVerdict | None':
197	    """The node, or `None` — a verdict that cannot be proved is not placed. See
198	    `proven_verdict_or_failures` for the diagnostic form."""
199	    node, _fails = proven_verdict_or_failures(text, premise_ids, b)
200	    return node
201	
202	
203	@dataclass(frozen=True)

1262	def entailed_by_span(text: str, span: str, work: P.Work | None = None,
1263	                     min_overlap: float = 0.25) -> tuple[bool, str]:
1264	    """Is THIS text ENTAILED by THIS verbatim span? The span is the only evidence there is.
1265	
1266	    `claim` — the model's restatement — is not a parameter of this function and never will be. The old
1267	    gate read `src = f'{span} {claim}'`, and since the writer was handed only the claim, the chain was:
1268	    the model writes the claim -> the writer writes from the claim -> the gate checks the writing
1269	    against the claim. THE GATE VALIDATED THE MODEL AGAINST ITSELF.
1270	
1271	    Three layers, in order:
1272	
1273	      1. A DETERMINISTIC PRE-FILTER that may only REJECT (numbers+units, a strict direction opposition, a
1274	         content floor). It is cheap and certain and it kills the obvious fabrication offline. Surviving
1275	         it means "not yet rejected", never "entailed".
1276	      2. THE ONE SOUND DETERMINISTIC ADMIT: a NON-numeric clause that is a contiguous window of ONE
1277	         source clause, dropping no meaning-flipping edge qualifier (`contiguous_window_admit`). This is
1278	         the only deletion a span cannot lie by, so it admits even with the judge down — the true finding
1279	         reaches the page during a model blip instead of being starved. Everything else falls through.
1280	      3. THE JUDGE, consulted on the WHOLE clause vs the WHOLE span for EVERY clause that survives (1) and
1281	         is not admitted by (2), not conditioned on any word list. ONLY 'ENTAILED' admits; 'NOT_ENTAILED'
1282	         and 'UNCERTAIN' — and a judge that cannot be reached — REJECT. This is the line that catches the
1283	         synonym sign-flip ('plunged'), the magnitude fabrication ('doubled'), the scope swap
1284	         ('worldwide') and the modality flip ('causes'), none of which any lexicon here names.
1285	    """
1286	    src = (span or '').lower()
1287	    if not src:
1288	        return False, 'NO_SPAN'
1289	    ctext = (text or '').lower()
1290	
1291	    # ============ DETERMINISTIC PRE-FILTER — REJECT-ONLY. It may kill; it may never admit. ============
1292	    # (A) NUMBERS + UNITS. Every quantity in the clause must stand alone in the span with the SAME unit.
1293	    #     No single-digit exemption. No year exemption. "1.5 points" does not support "1.5 percent".
1294	    span_q = quantities_in(src)
1295	    for num, unit in quantities_in(ctext):
1296	        if not _quantity_supported(num, unit, src, span_q):
1297	            return False, f'NUMBER_OR_UNIT_NOT_IN_SPAN:{num}{(" " + unit) if unit else ""}'
1298	    # A bare integer with no recognised unit (e.g. a fabricated "2021 regions") is still a quantity.
1299	    for num in numbers_in(ctext):
1300	        if not number_stands_alone(num, src):
1301	            return False, f'NUMBER_NOT_IN_SPAN:{num}'
1302	
1303	    # (B) DIRECTION / POLARITY. Reject only a strict opposition — clause one way, span the other way.
1304	    #     This catches the IN-lexicon flip ("fell" vs "rose") without a model call; the OUT-of-lexicon
1305	    #     flip ("plunged", "slid", "weakened") is left for the judge, which is the whole point of (2).
1306	    cw = set(re.findall(r'[a-z]+', ctext))
1307	    sw = set(re.findall(r'[a-z]+', src))
1308	    c_up, c_down = bool(cw & _UP), bool(cw & _DOWN)
1309	    s_up, s_down = bool(sw & _UP), bool(sw & _DOWN)
1310	    if (c_down and not c_up and s_up and not s_down) or (c_up and not c_down and s_down and not s_up):
1311	        return False, 'DIRECTION_CONTRADICTS_SPAN'
1312	
1313	    # (C) CONTENT FLOOR. A clause whose content words are largely absent from the span is unsupported.
1314	    words = {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', ctext)} - _STOP
1315	    if work:
1316	        words -= {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', (work.venue or '').lower())}
1317	        words -= {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', ' '.join(work.authors or []).lower())}
1318	    src_words = {w for w in re.findall(r'[a-z]{4,}', src)}
1319	    if words and len(words & src_words) / len(words) < min_overlap:
1320	        return False, 'CONTENT_NOT_IN_SPAN'
1321	
1322	    # ============ NO DETERMINISTIC ADMIT IN THE FINDING LANE (SOL V11) ================================
1323	    # The old `contiguous_window_admit` concluded ADMIT for a contiguous word-window of ONE source clause.
1324	    # SOL V11 — THE ANTI-OVERFIT LAW: clause-boundary detection may ONLY reject or route, NEVER authorize
1325	    # admission. A window is still rendered as a FINDING ('<Author> show that ...'), and even a
1326	    # BYTE-IDENTICAL run of a span may be a HYPOTHESIS, a QUESTION, or someone else's claim — the speech
1327	    # act is dropped the moment a window is upgraded into a finding. So the finding lane has NO
1328	    # deterministic admit: the ONLY thing that says ENTAILED is the judge, and NOT_ENTAILED, UNCERTAIN and
1329	    # an unreachable judge all fail closed. The single structural ADMIT in the whole file now lives in the
1330	    # QUOTATION lane (a `Quotation` node: payload byte-identical to the WHOLE evidence unit, rendered
1331	    # NEUTRALLY as '<Author> writes: "..."', whose only guarantee is 'these bytes occur in this source').
1332	
1333	    # ============ THE JUDGE — WHOLE CLAUSE vs WHOLE SPAN, EVERY TIME. ONLY 'ENTAILED' ADMITS. ==========
1334	    verdict, why = _semantic_judge(text, span)
1335	    if verdict != _ENTAILED:
1336	        return False, f'JUDGE_{verdict}' + (f' :: {why}' if why else '')

1041	    verdict admits. EMPIRICAL, UNCERTAIN, an unparseable reply and an unreachable judge ALL FAIL CLOSED

1043	    longer decides whether the judge runs."""

1878	            #     A `ProvenVerdict` SKIPS this judge — and ONLY this judge — because its text is one of
1879	            #     the deterministic planner's own CLOSED canonical templates (never model prose), and its
1880	            #     claim class has just been re-proved and re-matched to its declared operation above. The
1881	            #     judge is the one remaining LIVE-MODEL step, and it was a silent second path to `0
1882	            #     verdicts placed`; an ordinary `Owned` synthesis (whose text a model wrote) still routes
1883	            #     to it and still fails closed.
1884	            if not isinstance(n, ProvenVerdict):
1885	                why_emp = _owned_frame_empirical(n.text)
1886	                if why_emp:
1887	                    return [Failure(i, 'Owned', 'SYNTHESIS_SMUGGLES_A_FINDING', why_emp)]
1888	            return []
1889	        # 3b. A ProvenVerdict IS a proof-carrying synthesis; without premises it can prove nothing and is
1890	        #     a forgery. It may not fall through to the premise-free frame lane and ship as a "frame".
1891	        if isinstance(n, ProvenVerdict):
1892	            return [Failure(i, 'ProvenVerdict', 'PROVEN_VERDICT_HAS_NO_PREMISES', str(n.text[:70]))]
1893	        # 4. A FRAME sentence: licensed by nothing, so it may assert NO PARTICULAR. Digits and source
1894	        #    names are refused above; a spelled quantity, a magnitude word ("fatal", "doubled"), or a
1895	        #    novel named entity ("...the Fourth Industrial Revolution...") is refused HERE. This closes
1896	        #    the premise-free OWNED bypass — the lane the released abstract used (SOL_BURN_V10 §2).
1897	        why2 = _owned_frame_particular(n.text)
1898	        if why2:
1899	            return [Failure(i, 'Owned', why2, n.text[:70])]

1052	
1053	@dataclass
1054	class SourceClause:
1055	    card_id: str                 # BOUND BY CARD_ID. Never by surname -- 'Acemoglu' names two papers here.
1056	    clause_text: str             # the VERBATIM SPAN. Immutable. This is the evidence.
1057	    attribution: str = ''        # the exact in-prose citation form the writer must use
1058	    unit: str = ''
1059	    method: str = ''
1060	    horizon: str = ''
1061	    figures: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)   # span-verified figures the writer MUST print

1065	class SentenceIR:
1066	    voice: str                                   # 'attributed' | 'owned'
1067	    text: str                                    # owned: the proposition, pre-validated. attributed: the brief.
1068	    source_clauses: list[SourceClause] = field(default_factory=list)
1069	    premise_card_ids: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
1070	    role: str = ''                               # thesis | evidence | verdict | boundary | bridge
1071	    gate: str = ''                               # which bar this sentence has ALREADY cleared
1072	    proof: RelationProof | None = None           # RUNG 4: the verdict's proof object (owned verdicts only)

1075	def _corpus_surnames(cards: list[dict]) -> set[str]:
1076	    return {a for c in cards for a in (c.get('authors') or []) if len(a) >= 4}

1079	def owned_is_safe(text: str, surnames: set[str], premise_blob: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:
1080	    """AN OWNED SENTENCE MAY NEVER SILENTLY INHERIT A SOURCE ATTRIBUTION.
1081	
1082	    The reviewer's voice is licensed to be NON-ENTAILED -- that is what insight is. The price of that
1083	    licence is that it names nobody and carries no particular. The moment an owned sentence names a
1084	    source it has become an ATTRIBUTED sentence with no evidence behind it, which is a fabrication.
1085	    Checked against EVERY surname in the corpus, not merely the ones in this bundle.
1086	    """
1087	    for s in sorted(surnames):
1088	        if re.search(rf'\b{re.escape(s)}\b', text, re.I):
1089	            return False, f'OWNED_NAMES_A_SOURCE:{s}'
1090	    if re.search(r'\d', text):
1091	        return False, 'OWNED_CARRIES_A_NUMBER'
1092	    # THE SAME STRUCTURAL PREDICATE the deterministic gate uses (Sol P1). owned_is_safe no longer imports
1093	    # a SECOND allow-list (the old `SAFE_CAPS`) — a caps rule that lived in two places is a caps rule that
1094	    # can disagree with itself. `caps_earned` decides here exactly as it decides in `validate()`.
1095	    from synthesis_contract import (SPELLED_QTY, FORECAST, UNIVERSAL, CAUSAL_IMPORT, CAP_TOKEN,
1096	                                    caps_earned, _content_lemmas)
1097	    if SPELLED_QTY.search(text):
1098	        return False, f'OWNED_CARRIES_A_SPELLED_QUANTITY:{SPELLED_QTY.search(text).group(0)}'
1099	    if FORECAST.search(text):
1100	        return False, f'OWNED_FORECASTS:{FORECAST.search(text).group(0)}'
1101	    if UNIVERSAL.search(text):
1102	        return False, f'OWNED_OVERCLAIMS:{UNIVERSAL.search(text).group(0)}'
1103	    if CAUSAL_IMPORT.search(text):
1104	        return False, f'OWNED_IMPORTS_A_MECHANISM:{CAUSAL_IMPORT.search(text).group(0)}'
1105	    sent_initial = {m.group(1) for m in re.finditer(r"(?:^|[.!?;:]\s+)([A-Z][A-Za-z&.\-']*)", text)}
1106	    blob_low = premise_blob.lower()
1107	    prem_caps = set(CAP_TOKEN.findall(premise_blob))
1108	    prem_stems = _content_lemmas(premise_blob)
1109	    for tok in CAP_TOKEN.findall(text):

1109	    for tok in CAP_TOKEN.findall(text):
1110	        if tok in sent_initial:
1111	            continue                       # orthography, not an entity
1112	        part = re.sub(r"'s$", '', tok)
1113	        if caps_earned(part, premise_caps=prem_caps, premise_blob_lower=blob_low,
1114	                       premise_stems=prem_stems):
1115	            continue
1116	        return False, f'OWNED_NEW_ENTITY:{tok}'
1117	    return True, ''
